children.heading: Regulatory authority
children.label: a Regulatory authority
children.text: The Secretary of the Treasury shall prescribe rules implementing the principles contained in subsection (b) for determining the origin of textiles and apparel products. Such rules shall be promulgated in final form not later than .July 1, 1995

children.label: A
children.text: the product is wholly obtained or produced in that country, territory, or possession;

children.label: i
children.text: the constituent staple fibers are spun in that country, territory, or possession, or

children.label: ii
children.text: the continuous filament is extruded in that country, territory, or possession;

children.label: C
children.text: the product is a fabric, including a fabric classified under chapter 59 of the HTS, and the constituent fibers, filaments, or yarns are woven, knitted, needled, tufted, felted, entangled, or transformed by any other fabric-making process in that country, territory, or possession; or

children.label: D
children.text: the product is any other textile or apparel product that is wholly assembled in that country, territory, or possession from its component pieces.

children.label: i
children.text: the origin of a good that is classified under one of the following HTS headings or subheadings shall be determined under subparagraph (A), (B), or (C) of paragraph (1), as appropriate: 5609, 5807, 5811, 6209.20.50.40, 6213, 6214, 6301, 6302, 6303, 6304, 6305, 6306, 6307.10, 6307.90, 6308, or 9404.90; and

children.label: ii
children.text: a textile or apparel product which is knit to shape shall be considered to originate in, and be the growth, product, or manufacture of, the country, territory, or possession in which it is knit.

children.label: B
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1)(C), fabric classified under the HTS as of silk, cotton, man-made fiber, or vegetable fiber shall be considered to originate in, and be the growth, product, or manufacture of, the country, territory, or possession in which the fabric is both dyed and printed when accompanied by two or more of the following finishing operations: bleaching, shrinking, fulling, napping, decating, permanent stiffening, weighting, permanent embossing, or moireing.

children.label: C
children.text: Notwithstanding paragraph (1)(D), goods classified under HTS heading 6117.10, 6213.00, 6214.00, 6302.22, 6302.29, 6302.52, 6302.53, 6302.59, 6302.92, 6302.93, 6302.99, 6303.92, 6303.99, 6304.19, 6304.93, 6304.99, 9404.90.85, or 9404.90.95, except for goods classified under such headings as of cotton or of wool or consisting of fiber blends containing 16 percent or more by weight of cotton, shall be considered to originate in, and be the growth, product, or manufacture of, the country, territory, or possession in which the fabric is both dyed and printed when accompanied by two or more of the following finishing operations: bleaching, shrinking, fulling, napping, decating, permanent stiffening, weighting, permanent embossing, or moireing.

children.label: A
children.text: the country, territory, or possession in which the most important assembly or manufacturing process occurs, or

children.label: B
children.text: if the origin of the good cannot be determined under subparagraph (A), the last country, territory, or possession in which important assembly or manufacturing occurs.

children.heading: Components cut in the United States
children.label: 4 Components cut in the United States
i
children.text: shall not be included in the dutiable value of such article, and

children.label: ii
children.text: may be applied toward determining the percentage referred to in General Note 7(b)(i)(B) of the HTS, subject to the limitation provided in that note.

children.label: i
children.text: the components after exportation from the United States, and

children.label: ii
children.text: the article itself before importation into the United States

children.heading: Exception for United States-Israel Free Trade Agreement
children.label: 5 Exception for United States-Israel Free Trade Agreement
children.text: This section shall not affect, for purposes of the customs laws and administration of quantitative restrictions, the status of goods that, under rulings and administrative practices in effect immediately before , would have originated in, or been the growth, product, or manufacture of, a country that is a party to an agreement with the United States establishing a free trade area, which entered into force before . For such purposes, such rulings and administrative practices that were applied, immediately before , to determine the origin of textile and apparel products covered by such agreement shall continue to apply after , and on and after the effective date described in subsection (c), unless such rulings and practices are modified by the mutual consent of the parties to the agreement.December 8, 1994January 1, 1987December 8, 1994December 8, 1994
the contract for the sale of such goods to the United States is entered into before ;July 20, 1994
all of the material terms of sale in such contract, including the price and quantity of the goods, are fixed and determinable before ;July 20, 1994

a copy of the contract is filed with the Commissioner of Customs within 60 days after , together with a certification that the contract meets the requirements of paragraphs (1) and (2); andDecember 8, 1994
the goods are entered, or withdrawn from warehouse, for consumption on or before .January 1, 1998
